REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR: 1) APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER, AND; 2) PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IN AID OF RECEIVER IS OFF CALENDAR. NO VALID PROOF OF SERVICE FOR THE MOTION ON FILE AS OF MAY 26, 2022. THE POS IS NOT EXECUTED BY A PERSON PURPORTED TO HAVE ACCOMPLISHED THE SERVICE ("CAUSING THE DOCUMENTS TO BE PLACED IN A SEALED FEDEX ENVELOPE AND THE ENVELOPE TO BE DELIVERED TO FEDEX" AND "I AM READILY FAMILIAR WITH THE FIRM'S PRACTICE OF COLLECTION AND PROCESSING CORRESPONDENCE FOR MAILING. UNDER THAT PRACTICE IT WOULD BE DEPOSITED WITH THE U.S. POSTAL SERVICE ON THAT SAME DAY WITH POSTAGE THEREON FULLY PREPAID IN THE ORDINARY COURSE OF BUSINESS.") AND CONTRARY TO THE DECLARATION THAT SERVICE WAS ACCOMPLISHED BY CAUSING TRANSMISSION BY ELECTRONIC MAIL TO THE "EMAIL ADDRESS SET FORTH BELOW" THERE IS NO E-MAIL ADDRESS SET FORTH. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT THERE IS NO PROOF OF SERVICE FOR THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT ON FILE AS OF MAY 26, 2022. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For: (1) Appointment Of Receiver, And; (2) Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of Receiver
Off Calendar May-27-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Oliver Gold, 310.788.9900 of Perkins Coie, LLP appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff Wells fargo Bank.
No other appearances.

Mr. Gold argues that the POS was filed yesterday and is valid.

Argued. The Court rules as follows: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR: 1) APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER, AND; 2) PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IN AID OF RECEIVER is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service for the motion on file as of May 26, 2022. The POS is not executed by a person purported to have accomplished the service ("causing the documents to be placed in a sealed FEDEX envelope and the envelope to be delivered to Fedex" and "I am readily familiar with the firm's practice of collection and processing correspondence for mailing. Under that practice it would be deposited with the U.S. Postal Service on that same day with postage thereon fully prepaid in the ordinary course of business.") and contrary to the declaration that service was accomplished by causing transmission by electronic mail to the "email address set forth below" there is no e-mail address set forth. The Court further notes that there is no proof of service for the summons and complaint on file as of May 26, 2022.
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
